page: Microsoft Azure FX16ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 260 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations. The system is equipped with 336.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per physical core.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance, with the Geekbench single-core score reaching 2143.0 and single-threaded gzip compression processing at 34.37 GB/sec. Text processing performance is in the top tier, scoring 2983.0 on Geekbench. For artificial intelligence workloads, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 415.01 tokens per second using a small 135M LLM, while medium 7B model prompt processing operates at 113.33 tokens per second. However, the PassMark memory score is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% at 1533.55, despite average uncached memory read bandwidth of 81.55 GB/sec.

This server configuration does not include local storage, requiring reliance on external premium storage options. There are also zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with the instance. Given the combination of high-frequency Intel Xeon cores, extensive memory capacity, and strong single-core execution, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, text processing, and CPU-based language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure FX16ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs from an 8-core Intel Xeon 8573C processor clocked at 4.2 GHz, supported by 336.0 GB of RAM. The architecture includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong single-core execution. However, memory subsystem performance shows some limitations, with a weak PassMark memory score. The server lacks local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, relying entirely on remote premium storage. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive database operations, text processing pipelines, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs on a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 336 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16ms_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 vCPUs, 336 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for demanding database and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 336 GB of RAM for memory-intensive applications.
Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz.
Top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation.
No local storage included, requiring external premium storage solutions.
Best suited for memory-heavy databases, text processing, and CPU-based inference.
